Politics.headline: Trump Says He'll Have the 'Honor' of 'Taking' Cuba: 'I Can Do Anything'
Politics.detail: Amid a U.S. oil blockade, President Trump commented he would have the 'honor' of 'taking' Cuba, following the country's electrical grid collapse on Monday which caused an island-wide blackout.

Politics.headline: U.S. intelligence says Iran's regime is consolidating power
Politics.detail: Despite withering airstrikes, U.S. intelligence officials predict a weakened but more hard-line government in Tehran, backed by the powerful Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps security forces.

Politics.headline: Judge halts RFK Jr.'s vaccine overhaul, citing flawed process
Politics.detail: A judge has temporarily blocked federal health officials from cutting the number of vaccines recommended for every child, stating that U.S. Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. likely violated federal procedures in revamping a key vaccine advisory committee.

Politics.headline: Carney, Starmer meet amid deepening Middle East crisis
Politics.detail: Prime Minister Mark Carney and U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer met in London to discuss the escalating Middle East war and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, condemning Iran's attacks and warning of mounting civilian and economic costs.

Politics.headline: Republicans resist calls for Iran war hearings, creating a new standoff with Democrats
Politics.detail: Republicans in Congress are resisting calls for Iran war hearings, creating a new standoff with Democrats amid concerns over religious hatred amplified online and echoed by some Republican officials.

Finance & Economy.headline: Cruise lines face fuel cost surge as oil prices jump on Iran tensions
Finance & Economy.detail: Cruise lines are experiencing a surge in fuel costs due to a 35% jump in oil prices amid the Iran conflict, potentially impacting profits during the peak summer booking season.

Finance & Economy.headline: US stocks climb to their best day since the Iran war began after oil prices ease
Finance & Economy.detail: A drop in oil prices helped send the U.S. stock market to its best day since the war in Iran began, with the S&P 500 climbing 1%.

Finance & Economy.headline: Canada's annual inflation rate fell to 1.8% in February, war's impact not yet reflected
Finance & Economy.detail: Statistics Canada reported that Canada's annual inflation rate decreased to 1.8 percent in February; the impact of the ongoing war is not yet reflected in these figures.

Finance & Economy.headline: Europe looks to step back from U.S. Big Tech
Finance & Economy.detail: Europe is considering measures to reduce its reliance on major U.S. technology companies.

Finance & Economy.headline: Global Economy: Steady amid Divergent Forces
Finance & Economy.detail: The IMF projects global growth at 3.3 percent for 2026 and 3.2 percent for 2027, noting that technology investment, fiscal and monetary support, and private sector adaptability are offsetting trade policy shifts.

Technology.headline: Nvidia expects to make $1 trillion from AI chips through 2027
Technology.detail: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang predicted that its flagship AI processors would help generate $1 trillion in sales through 2027 and unveiled plans to push deeper into central processing units, even developing chips for data centers in outer space. The company introduced semiconductors made with technology acquired from startup Groq.

Technology.headline: Salesforce stock buyback to saddle company with debt until 2066
Technology.detail: Salesforce CEO Benioff says debt is a great way to use capital correctly as the company initiates a stock buyback program.

Technology.headline: Nvidia's DLSS 5 promises to bring you out the other side of the uncanny valley
Technology.detail: The latest generation of Nvidia's AI image enhancer brings characters to life.

Technology.headline: Nvidia wraps its NemoClaw around OpenClaw for the sake of security
Technology.detail: Nvidia CEO insists OpenClaw is the operating system for personal AI.

Technology.headline: AI finally delivers those elusive productivity gains... for cybercriminals
Technology.detail: Interpol says fraud schemes using AI are 4.5x more profitable.

Science.headline: New study raises concerns about AI chatbots fueling delusional thinking
Science.detail: A recent study suggests that using AI chatbots may contribute to delusional thinking in individuals.

Science.headline: Please drive carefully: scientists plan to transport volatile antimatter for first time
Science.detail: Scientists are preparing to transport volatile antimatter, requiring careful handling and safety measures.

Science.headline: Exceptionally rare sighting of planets colliding may shed light on the crash that formed the moon
Science.detail: Astronomers have observed a rare collision of planets around a distant star, potentially providing insights into the ancient impact that formed Earth's moon.

Science.headline: Extreme heat is cutting the time people can safely be active outdoors
Science.detail: A new study finds that extreme heat is reducing the amount of time people can safely engage in outdoor activities.

Science.headline: Single-cell data reveal a cellular 'developmental hourglass' in vertebrate embryos
Science.detail: Researchers have discovered a cellular 'developmental hourglass' in vertebrate embryos using single-cell data, providing new insights into embryonic development.

Health.headline: Meningitis in Kent: Two young people dead and 11 seriously ill in outbreak
Health.detail: An outbreak of meningitis in Kent has resulted in the tragic deaths of two young individuals, with 11 others reported to be seriously ill.

Health.headline: Japan becomes first to approve stem cell therapies for Parkinson's
Health.detail: Japan has become the first country to approve stem cell therapies for Parkinson's disease, marking a significant advancement in the treatment of this neurodegenerative disorder.

Health.headline: New Telehealth Standards: The Upshot for Australian Doctors
Health.detail: New telehealth standards have been released, impacting how Australian doctors provide remote care to their patients.

Health.headline: US cigarette smoking drops to record low, vaping and nicotine pouch use surges
Health.detail: While cigarette smoking in the U.S. has reached a record low, there's a corresponding surge in the use of vaping products and nicotine pouches.

Health.headline: Doctors Argue 'Provider' Blurs Clinical Roles
Health.detail: The American College of Physicians argues that the term 'provider' obscures the distinctions between nurse practitioners and physicians, potentially confusing patients and undermining the recognition of specialized expertise.

Sports.headline: Chiefs reportedly trade for Jets quarterback Fields with Mahomes's return date uncertain
Sports.detail: The Kansas City Chiefs have acquired quarterback Justin Fields from the New York Jets, with the return date of Patrick Mahomes uncertain. Fields expressed his preference to join the Chiefs, marking his third team in the league.

Sports.headline: Young holds off Fitzpatrick on final hole to win Players Championship
Sports.detail: Cameron Young secured victory at the Players Championship, narrowly defeating Matt Fitzpatrick on the final hole.

Sports.headline: Antonelli on pole for F1's Chinese Grand Prix after teammate Russell wins sprint
Sports.detail: Antonelli secured pole position for the F1 Chinese Grand Prix after his teammate Russell won the sprint. Antonelli takes first F1 win with Mercedes one-two in China; The 19-year-old Italian driver became the second-youngest race winner in the sport's history after four-times world champion Max Verstappen, who took his first victory with Red Bull at 18 in 2016.

Sports.headline: Junior rugby league registrations skyrocket across Western Australia
Sports.detail: Junior rugby league registrations in Western Australia have surged by 23% in the 13-15 age bracket and 17% overall (ages 6-18) as the Perth Bears prepare to join the NRL in 2027. This increase leads to the addition of three new teams in the NRL WA "Fuel to Go & Play" Premiership.

Sports.headline: Enforcer's $1m payday as final call on his future made in multi-club battle: Transfer Whispers
Sports.detail: The former Queensland Origin prop, Francis Molo, has re-signed with the Dolphins for a deal worth around $1 million, turning down potentially $300,000 to remain at the club until the end of the 2028 season.

Entertainment.headline: Oscars 2026: 'One Battle After Another' wins best picture
Entertainment.detail: Paul Thomas Anderson's 'One Battle After Another' was crowned best picture at the 98th Academy Awards, marking a significant triumph for the director. Jessie Buckley and Michael B. Jordan won best actress and actor respectively.

Entertainment.headline: Dharmendra added to In Memoriam segment online after snub during Oscars 2026
Entertainment.detail: Following criticism, Bollywood legend Dharmendra, who passed away last year, was included in the online version of the Oscars' In Memoriam segment. The initial snub had drawn disappointment from fans and industry peers.

Entertainment.headline: Robert Redford's Cause of Death Updates: How Did the Actor Die?
Entertainment.detail: Robert Redford, the iconic actor, passed away at the age of 89, prompting tributes during the Oscars ceremony. Details regarding the cause of death are still emerging.

Entertainment.headline: Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda's dreamy Haldi ceremony turns into a colourful Holi-like celebration, see pics
Entertainment.detail: Photos from Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda's Haldi ceremony reveal a vibrant, Holi-like celebration. The actress shared that the event was filled with color and games, with even Vijay joining 'Team Bride'.

Entertainment.headline: BTS documentary film 'BTS: The Return' to be released March 27
Entertainment.detail: A new BTS documentary film, titled 'BTS: The Return,' is set to be released on March 27, offering fans another glimpse into the popular K-pop group's journey. The film is expected to showcase behind-the-scenes footage and interviews.

Crime & Justice.headline: Owners and CEO of Wholesale Pharmaceutical Company Sentenced for Distributing More Than $92M of Black-Market HIV Drugs
Crime & Justice.detail: The owners and CEO of a wholesale pharmaceutical company have been sentenced for distributing over $92 million worth of black-market HIV drugs. The sentencing highlights efforts to combat illegal drug distribution and protect public health.

Crime & Justice.headline: Former Syrian Assad Prison Official Convicted of Torture and Immigration Fraud
Crime & Justice.detail: A former Syrian prison official under Assad has been convicted of torture and immigration fraud. This conviction marks a significant step in holding individuals accountable for human rights abuses.

Crime & Justice.headline: Maryland Man Sentenced for Mailing Threatening Communications to Jewish Institutions and Civil Rights Violations
Crime & Justice.detail: A Maryland man has been sentenced for mailing threatening communications to Jewish institutions and committing civil rights violations. The sentencing underscores the commitment to combating hate crimes and protecting religious freedom.

Crime & Justice.headline: The crime of political distortion has no accuser
Crime & Justice.detail: A new crime of distorting the law in Korea is causing concern that even conscientious prosecutors and judges could face lawsuits and complaints, potentially overwhelming the judicial system. Critics argue that this reform risks creating a litigation crisis instead of protecting citizens’ legal rights.

Crime & Justice.headline: Repeat offender arrested by Wetaskiwin and Maskwacis RCMP
Crime & Justice.detail: RCMP in Wetaskiwin and Maskwacis, Alberta, Canada teamed up to arrest Bobby Larenze Bull, a repeat offender, after he fled from police in a vehicle with an obstructed license plate. Bull was charged with flight from a peace officer and dangerous operation of a motor vehicle and has a court date set for March 17, 2026.

Education.headline: Education Ministry says no PdPR for now, cites Covid-19 setback to students
Education.detail: The Ministry of Education (MOE) in Malaysia has decided against implementing home-based teaching and learning (PdPR) for the time being, citing the negative impact it had on students' learning during the Covid-19 pandemic. The ministry prioritizes recovering from learning disruptions and ensuring the quality of student learning.

Education.headline: School Assembly News Headlines (March 17): Top National, World, Sports Updates
Education.detail: A compilation of top news headlines suitable for school assemblies, covering national and international events, as well as sports updates. These headlines provide a snapshot of current affairs for students.

Education.headline: Agency allocates NT$90 million for tourism education
Education.detail: Taiwan's Tourism Administration has allocated NT$90 million to fund educational sessions for tourism professionals and develop e-commerce management systems, aiming to upgrade digital capabilities and service quality within the travel industry. Subsidies are available for tourism associations and non-profits to host educational sessions.

Education.headline: Why the way we sound still determines who is heard
Education.detail: An article discussing how a person's accent and way of speaking can still affect how they are perceived and heard in various settings. This highlights the importance of inclusive communication and overcoming biases related to speech.

Education.headline: CBSE issues warning to Class 10, 12 evaluators over social media posts
Education.detail: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) has warned teachers evaluating Class 10 and Class 12 board exam papers against posting related content on social media. This is to maintain the integrity and confidentiality of the evaluation process.

Lifestyle.headline: Shilpa Shetty Reveals Her 'Secret To Staying Calm' Before Work
Lifestyle.detail: The Bollywood actress shares her tip for maintaining composure before starting her workday.

Lifestyle.headline: Big W launches organisational 'game changer' in $199 digital e-calendar and shoppers are losing their minds
Lifestyle.detail: An Australian retailer has released a new digital e-calendar device priced at $199, which has become highly sought after by shoppers for its organizational capabilities.

Lifestyle.headline: Apple unveils second-generation AirPods Max, more than five years after debut
Lifestyle.detail: The new headphones will be available to order from Mar 25 in more than 30 countries, including Singapore, with retail availability beginning early next month.

Lifestyle.headline: Weight loss drugs proposed to tackle childhood obesity crisis
Lifestyle.detail: There is a proposition to use weight loss drugs as a means to address the growing issue of childhood obesity.

Lifestyle.headline: Categories announced for Harrogate Dog Show 2026
Lifestyle.detail: The categories for the Harrogate Dog Show 2026 have been revealed.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Sitting ducks
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The world is grappling with soaring oil prices as an offshoot of the United States and Israel's conflict with Iran, leaving thousands of Filipino seafarers in the war-torn Middle East in a precarious situation.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: When the Joke Isn't Funny: Misogyny Hidden Behind Laughter
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This opinion piece discusses how misogyny can be masked behind humor.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: No, MAGA is not divided on the Iran war.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This article analyzes the unified stance of the MAGA movement regarding the ongoing war with Iran.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Brendan Carr, minister of truth
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This Washington Post editorial discusses Brendan Carr.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Hiking the minimum wage puts robots over people.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Washington Post Editorial Board argues that increasing the minimum wage favors automation over human labor.

World.headline: Trump Urges Allies to Secure Strait of Hormuz, Dubai Flights Suspended
World.detail: Amid the ongoing US-Israeli war with Iran, President Trump has called on allies to secure the Strait of Hormuz, a vital energy route, while Dubai flights have been suspended following recent security incidents and attacks in the region. The UAE's air defenses have reportedly intercepted numerous ballistic missiles and drones launched from Iran.

World.headline: Cuba Suffers Nationwide Blackout as Fuel Supplies Dwindle
World.detail: Cuba's electrical grid experienced a total collapse, leaving 10 million residents without power, as the country grapples with a de facto U.S. oil blockade and crumbling infrastructure. The Ministry of Energy and Mines is investigating the cause of the failure.

World.headline: European Leaders Reject Military Involvement in Strait of Hormuz
World.detail: As tensions escalate in the Middle East, European leaders are resisting calls to send warships to assist in reopening the Strait of Hormuz, distancing themselves from the US approach to the Iran war. Meanwhile, Trump expresses frustration with allies over the Strait of Hormuz operation.

World.headline: US-Iran Direct Communication Channel Reactivated
World.detail: A direct communications channel between U.S. and Iranian officials has been reactivated in recent days, marking the first known direct contact since the United States and Israel launched massive attacks on Iran. The communication is between U.S. envoy Steve Witkoff and Iranian Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi.

World.headline: Global Military Leaders Meet to Strengthen Regional Security
World.detail: Global military leaders convened to discuss and reinforce strategies for regional security. The meeting underscores the ongoing efforts to address and mitigate escalating tensions and conflicts around the world.

Zimbabwe.headline: 'Amendment No. 3 Bill strengthens institutions'
Zimbabwe.detail: The Attorney-General, Mrs Virginia Mabiza, has clarified public confusion over the Constitution of Zimbabwe (Amendment No. 3) Bill, stating that it strengthens institutions.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zanu PF wants database for cadres
Zimbabwe.detail: The Zanu PF provincial leadership must maintain a comprehensive database of qualified and eligible cadres for deployment into Government, ensuring the country is served by patriotic and disciplined citizens.

Zimbabwe.headline: Competition Commission probes CBZ's tech firm acquisition
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe's largest financial services group, CBZ Holdings Limited, intends to expand its footprint beyond traditional banking through acquisition, which has prompted an investigation by the Competition Commission.

Zimbabwe.headline: RBZ seeks business buy-in for new ZiG notes
Zimbabwe.detail: The Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe is engaging with the business community as part of the process of introducing the new ZiG notes.

Zimbabwe.headline: Rwodzi Scoops Top Minister Award at Performance Evaluations
Zimbabwe.detail: Tourism and Hospitality Industry Minister Barbara Rwodzi emerged as the top-performing Minister in President Mnangagwa's 2025 Cabinet. Her exceptional contributions have been instrumental.
